fields.name: Newcastle Disease
fields.description: Highly contagious viral disease affecting all poultry. One of the biggest threats to Nairobi egg farmers. Spreads rapidly between flocks.
fields.symptoms: Sudden death,Twisted neck,Sneezing / coughing,Green diarrhoea,Reduced egg production,Loss of appetite,Paralysis,Ruffled feathers
fields.prevention: Vaccinate with La Sota strain at day 14 and booster at week 4. Maintain strict biosecurity. Only buy chicks from certified hatcheries.
fields.treatment: No cure — supportive care only. Isolate infected birds immediately. Report to county vet. Cull severely affected birds.

fields.name: Avian Influenza (Bird Flu)
fields.description: Notifiable disease. Must be reported to county veterinary office immediately. Can spread to humans (rare but serious).
fields.symptoms: Sudden death,Swollen face,Mucus discharge,Sneezing / coughing,Blood in droppings,Reduced egg production,Pale comb
fields.prevention: Strict biosecurity — no wild birds near farm. Disinfect all equipment. Restrict farm access. Buy from certified sources only.
fields.treatment: IMMEDIATELY contact county vet. Notify DVS Kenya. Do not move birds. Do not sell eggs from infected flock.

fields.name: Marek's Disease
fields.description: Viral disease causing tumours and paralysis. Common in layers. Vaccination at hatch is the only effective control.
fields.symptoms: Paralysis,Twisted neck,Loss of appetite,Ruffled feathers,Pale comb
fields.prevention: Vaccinate day-old chicks before leaving hatchery. Buy pre-vaccinated chicks from certified hatcheries only.
fields.treatment: No cure. Remove paralysed birds. Cull affected stock to prevent spread.

fields.name: Coccidiosis
fields.description: Parasitic gut disease — most common in young birds under 6 weeks. Caused by dirty, damp litter.
fields.symptoms: Blood in droppings,Diarrhoea,Loss of appetite,Ruffled feathers,Reduced egg production
fields.prevention: Keep litter dry. Avoid overcrowding. Medicate feed or water with coccidiostat. Clean drinkers daily.
fields.treatment: Treat with Amprolium (Amprol) in drinking water for 5 days. Supportive vitamins A and K.

fields.name: Infectious Bronchitis
fields.description: Highly contagious respiratory virus. Major cause of reduced egg production and poor shell quality in layers.
fields.symptoms: Sneezing / coughing,Mucus discharge,Reduced egg production,Loss of appetite
fields.prevention: Vaccinate at 1 and 4 weeks with IB vaccine. Good ventilation in housing. All-in all-out flock management.
fields.treatment: Supportive care — antibiotics to prevent secondary bacterial infection. Vitamins to boost immunity.
